images.original_text:
輿財圖記
人口乃公於客秋受事越仲春而賊已無蹤公何算無遺籌而疾如飛翰乃爾哉圖紀一書公不張大其事亦不詳著其畫然而簡稽之精識折執之大力籌較之苦心巳具犂然於茲矣敢弁斯言副之制刷以幷存實錄云
崇甲戌巡接直隸監察御史宛郡治生吳阿衡拜譔

persons.posture: Riding a horse, holding a staff in right hand, left hand gripping reins; body slightly leaning forward, weight centered over saddle, feet in stirrups; head turned left, observing surroundings.
persons.tactic: Commanding vanguard or leading troop through mountain pass; maintaining control while scanning terrain for threats.

persons.posture: Walking beside horse, left hand raised holding banner, right hand at side; upright stance, shoulders relaxed, weight evenly distributed between legs; gaze directed forward.
persons.tactic: Standard bearer signaling troop movement or morale; maintaining visual communication with rear units.

persons.posture: Standing behind banner-bearer, holding spear vertically with both hands near chest; feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, torso upright; eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Guardian or advance scout; prepared to engage or defend against ambush from flanks.

persons.posture: Mounted on horse, right arm extended holding sword aloft, left hand gripping mane; body angled slightly backward, legs braced for balance; head turned toward enemy direction.
persons.tactic: Leading charge or issuing command during combat; psychological intimidation through raised weapon.

persons.posture: On foot, holding halberd with both hands in low guard stance; knees bent, back slightly hunched, weight shifted to front leg; eyes fixed on approaching threat.
persons.tactic: Close-quarters defender or formation anchor; ready to block or counterattack in tight terrain.

persons.posture: Walking behind main group, carrying shield on left forearm and sword in right hand; posture alert, head turning left, steps cautious; weight balanced for quick maneuver.
persons.tactic: Flank protection or rearguard duty; prepared to intercept surprise attacks from side.

persons.posture: Partially obscured, standing with bow drawn; right arm pulled back, left hand steadying bowstring; legs in staggered stance, body twisted for power; eyes aimed downrange.
persons.tactic: Archery support or long-range suppression; providing covering fire while advancing troops.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, seated upright, right hand holding reins, left hand gripping spear shaft pointing forward; legs straddling saddle, weight balanced centrally over horse’s back.
persons.tactic: Forward charge formation — advancing with spear ready for thrusting attack, maintaining control of mount while preparing to strike enemy in close combat.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, slightly leaning forward, both hands gripping spear shaft near tip, spear held diagonally upward; body angled for momentum and striking power, feet planted firmly in stirrups.
persons.tactic: Offensive spear thrust — preparing to deliver a decisive piercing strike, using horse’s forward motion to enhance force and penetration.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, seated straight, right hand raised holding spear vertically above head, left hand gripping horse’s mane or reins; horse in mid-stride, body poised for rapid deployment.
persons.tactic: Defensive or preparatory stance — holding spear aloft to ward off incoming attacks or prepare for vertical strike, demonstrating readiness and tactical positioning within cavalry unit.

images.original_text:
兵法圖說
其一曰：山川險阻，軍行宜謹。前有峻嶺，後有追兵，當審地形，設伏以待。
又曰：騎卒衝鋒，步卒列陣，相輔而行，不可孤進。戰馬騰躍，士卒奮勇，氣勢如虹。

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance, left foot slightly ahead, right foot planted firmly. Right hand holds spear pointed forward; left hand grips the shaft near the tip. Body angled slightly to the left, weight distributed evenly between both feet. Eyes focused ahead, ready for engagement.
persons.tactic: Forward advance with spear formation, preparing to engage enemy forces ahead. This is a standard infantry charge posture used to maintain formation and pressure on advancing foes.

persons.posture: Slightly crouched stance, knees bent, body leaning forward. Both hands grip spear shaft near middle, spear held low and angled outward. Weight shifted onto front leg, rear leg braced for quick movement. Head turned toward center of formation.
persons.tactic: Flank support maneuver — maintaining defensive line while preparing to pivot or counterattack. This position allows for rapid response to flanking threats or enemy cavalry.

persons.posture: Standing upright, feet shoulder-width apart. Spear held vertically above head with both hands, ready to strike downward. Torso straight, shoulders relaxed, gaze fixed forward. Slight tension in legs indicates readiness to leap or thrust.
persons.tactic: High guard position for initiating a vertical thrust or overhead strike. Used when facing mounted opponents or to break through dense formations.

persons.posture: Seated atop horse in riding posture, legs gripping sides, one hand holding reins, other raised with sword drawn. Horse’s head turned left, ears alert. Rider leans slightly forward, eyes scanning battlefield ahead. Weight centered over saddle for balance during movement.
persons.tactic: Cavalry charge initiation — rider prepares to gallop forward with sword drawn for cutting or dismounting attack. This posture maximizes speed and impact upon contact.

persons.posture: Rider seated sideways on horse, body twisted to right side, sword held horizontally across chest. Horse moving at moderate pace, tail raised. Rider’s left arm extended for balance, right hand gripping sword hilt. Head turned backward, observing rear.
persons.tactic: Retreating or repositioning maneuver — rider uses horse’s momentum to pivot and redirect forces. Sword held defensively but ready to slash if pursued by enemy cavalry.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, left leg bent under body, right leg extended back. Both hands grip sword hilt, blade resting on ground. Head lowered, eyes fixed on ground level. Torso stable, core engaged for balance.
persons.tactic: Defensive kneeling stance — used to stabilize against cavalry charges or to set up ambushes. Sword positioned for immediate upward slash or lateral cut if enemy closes in.

persons.posture: Standing behind fallen figure, one leg forward, torso leaning slightly. Right hand raised as if gesturing or signaling; left hand holds sword sheathed. Face partially hidden, but posture suggests command or observation role.
persons.tactic: Commander’s positioning — this individual likely directs troop movements or signals tactical adjustments. The raised hand may indicate an order to advance, halt, or flank.

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot slightly advanced, body angled slightly to the right, weight balanced on both legs.右手持剑，剑尖斜指前方；左手自然垂于身侧。头微侧向左，目光注视前方。
persons.tactic: The person is in a ready combat stance, preparing to engage or defend. The sword is held in a defensive grip, suggesting an imminent offensive or counter-attack maneuver.

persons.posture: Walking forward with right foot leading, body upright, weight shifting forward.右手持剑，剑柄朝下，剑尖斜指地面；左手自然摆动于体侧。头正视前方，步伐稳健。
persons.tactic: This individual appears to be advancing cautiously, possibly scouting or approaching an opponent. The low-held sword suggests readiness for sudden engagement while maintaining control and balance.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, left leg forward, right leg back, body leaning slightly forward, holding a spear pointed diagonally upward. Right hand grips the spear shaft near the tip; left hand supports lower shaft. Weight distributed with front foot bearing more pressure.
persons.tactic: Charge attack formation — advancing to strike enemy while maintaining control of horse.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky terrain, left foot forward, right foot back in a stable stance. Right arm raised high holding a sword above head; left arm bent at side for balance. Torso twisted slightly to generate power for downward slash.
persons.tactic: High-angle slashing technique — preparing to decapitate or sever opponent’s upper body from elevated position.

persons.posture: Kneeling behind rocks, left knee down, right knee up, body crouched low. Both hands gripping a staff horizontally across chest, ready to thrust or parry. Eyes focused forward, alert for incoming attacks.
persons.tactic: Defensive ambush — using terrain cover to launch surprise counterattack with staff.

persons.posture: Crouching beside fallen comrade, one knee on ground, torso upright. Right hand holds bow drawn back; left hand steadies arrow nocked. Head turned sideways to aim, feet braced for stability.
persons.tactic: Sniping from cover — targeting high-value enemy from concealed position.

persons.posture: Standing atop hillside, legs apart in wide stance, arms extended outward holding a halberd. Right hand grips haft near blade; left hand supports mid-shaft. Body angled slightly backward to absorb recoil.
persons.tactic: Area control and intimidation — using height and weapon reach to dominate battlefield space.

persons.posture: Riding in formation across ridge, all facing same direction. Each rider holds spear or lance in right hand, left hand guiding reins. Bodies leaned forward in coordinated charge.
persons.tactic: Flanking maneuver — surrounding enemy forces by attacking from elevated flank.

images.original_text:
戰陣圖說
左：騎兵衝鋒，右：步兵列陣。山巒起伏，城池隱現，箭矢飛蝗，鼓角齊鳴。

persons.posture: Riding a galloping horse, body leaning forward, right arm extended holding a bow drawn back, left hand gripping reins. Feet planted in stirrups, weight shifted forward for momentum. Head turned slightly toward target, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Cavalry archery charge — aiming while moving at speed to disrupt enemy formation.

persons.posture: Same as above; mounted on horse, drawing bow with right hand, left hand guiding horse. Slight variation in angle suggests simultaneous firing or maneuvering within unit.
persons.tactic: Supporting archery fire during cavalry assault — maintaining pressure on enemy lines.

persons.posture: Standing on uneven ground near rocks, kneeling slightly with one knee on ground, drawing long bow with both hands. Body angled sideways to stabilize aim, feet braced apart for balance.
persons.tactic: Static ranged attack from defensive position — targeting high-value or exposed enemies.

persons.posture: Standing upright behind rocks, holding spear vertically with both hands, feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Torso facing forward, head alert, ready to thrust or parry.
persons.tactic: Defensive spear line — holding ground against advancing forces, prepared to counter charge.

persons.posture: Riding a horse, leaning forward with left arm extended, right leg bent in stirrup, body angled slightly leftward; appears to be charging or advancing in combat.
persons.tactic: Frontal cavalry assault, using momentum to overwhelm opponent.

persons.posture: On horseback, mid-air leap or fall, legs splayed, arms outstretched as if dismounting or being thrown; body horizontal to ground.
persons.tactic: Evasive maneuver or counterattack during mounted engagement.

persons.posture: On horseback, leaning backward with right arm raised, left leg dangling; appears to be retreating or dodging an attack.
persons.tactic: Defensive retreat or evasive riding technique under pressure.

persons.posture: Kneeling on rocky terrain, one knee on ground, upper body upright, right hand gripping weapon (not visible), left hand raised near chest.
persons.tactic: Defensive stance on uneven ground, preparing for close-quarters combat or ambush.

images.original_text:
籌財圖言
右土樵算勝圖六月二十五日漏下余同監紀陳謝二君並監軍曹憲副督左良玉周爾敬兵同趨土樵窩鄧總兵營中書剿賊之策是日賊衆先據勝地欲乘我疲勞餘賊眾直前奮勇殺賊賊知撫監道俱在行間相顧驚駭按營西走我兵追至下坡薄奮力擊之賊遁林縣裏村我兵追及巨礮轟發賊死傷甚衆

武藝圖譜總要
卷之二十三
山川景致圖

列戰圖記
山巒疊嶂，林木蔥蘢。左有茅屋隱於樹叢，右有騎士二人，各持長兵器，策馬疾馳，似欲突圍或追擊。一人前俯身，一手握柄，一手揚起；另一人後仰，雙手控韁，目視前方。

persons.posture: Rider on horse, leaning forward with left arm extended holding spear, right arm raised; body angled forward, weight shifted to front of horse, suggesting aggressive charge or thrusting motion.
persons.tactic: Offensive spear thrust while mounted, likely aiming to pierce or disrupt enemy formation.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, sitting upright with both hands gripping reins, head turned slightly forward, legs braced for control; body balanced and stable, indicating defensive or evasive maneuver.
persons.tactic: Controlled retreat or tactical repositioning while maintaining weapon readiness, possibly to regroup or flank.

images.original_text: 右山彪擊賊圍汲山西彪鎮密邇路藩園寢六月二十六日聞賊將往犯急檄催鄧鎮赴援而鄧鎮現在土樵窩不能一號而至余飛馬前往僅帶隨行官健百餘人夜至彪鎮值賊兵休息未定諸官健皆謂眾寡不敵不如暫退以待授師余意出其不意無難以寡破眾失此機會賊得秣馬勵兵以禦我經益多師未必遽勝一不勝而弛張矣突潞藩園寢為墟矣號令諸官健日此際不直衝賊營者斯果皆擠死大呼殺入賊營賊大驚潰散此天明賊兵復合而余已馳至輝城復率官健益以鄉兵數百撲賊賊不知我虛實相率遁入山園寢乃保

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing combat stance, left foot forward, right foot back; body slightly crouched with weight distributed evenly; right hand gripping sword hilt at waist level, left hand extended forward in a defensive gesture; head turned slightly to left, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Preparation for close-quarters combat, using the sword as both offensive and defensive tool while maintaining balance and readiness to advance or retreat.

persons.posture: Kneeling on right knee, left leg bent under body; upper torso upright, shoulders squared; right arm raised holding spear vertically above head, left arm extended outward for balance; gaze fixed forward with alert expression.
persons.tactic: Defensive positioning with spear ready to thrust or parry, utilizing low center of gravity for stability against approaching opponents.

persons.posture: Running stride with left leg forward, right leg trailing; arms pumping for momentum; torso leaning forward with chin tucked; eyes scanning ahead for threats or terrain obstacles.
persons.tactic: Rapid movement to flank or engage enemy from unexpected angle, combining speed with tactical positioning to disrupt formation.

persons.posture: Standing atop rocky outcrop, one foot planted firmly, other slightly raised; holding bow drawn with arrow nocked, body twisted sideways to aim; left arm supporting bowstring, right arm pulling back; head tilted upward, focused on target beyond frame.
persons.tactic: Long-range archery support, providing covering fire while observing battlefield dynamics from elevated position.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, holding a spear pointed forward, body leaning slightly forward, left leg braced in stirrup, right leg extended for balance; horse in mid-gallop with front legs lifted, tail flowing backward, indicating motion.
persons.tactic: Charge attack formation — rider advancing with spear ready to strike, exploiting momentum and height advantage.

persons.posture: Standing on uneven ground, one foot planted firmly, the other slightly back for stability; right arm raised holding a sword in guard position, left hand gripping the hilt near the base, body angled sideways to deflect or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Defensive stance with sword — preparing to parry or counter the incoming spear charge, utilizing terrain for tactical advantage.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, left hand gripping ground for support, right arm extended backward as if drawing a weapon or preparing to strike. Body angled forward, weight distributed low and stable, head turned toward the right, observing approaching figures.
persons.tactic: Defensive readiness in rugged terrain; preparing to counterattack or ambush.

persons.posture: Riding horse, leaning forward with both hands gripping reins, legs braced for control. Head turned slightly left, eyes focused ahead. Horse in mid-gallop, hooves lifted, tail streaming back.
persons.tactic: Cavalry charge or pursuit; maintaining momentum while scanning for enemy positions.

persons.posture: On horseback, right arm raised high holding a spear pointed forward, left arm bent across chest for balance. Torso twisted slightly, knees pressed against saddle, body leaning into the thrust.
persons.tactic: Spear thrusting technique — aiming to pierce or disrupt enemy formation during mounted combat.

persons.posture: Riding horse, left arm extended outward as if signaling or gesturing, right hand holding reins. Body upright, gaze directed forward. Horse’s head turned slightly left.
persons.tactic: Commander or scout role — directing troop movement or assessing battlefield layout.

persons.posture: On horseback, leaning sideways to the left, right arm drawn back as if releasing an arrow (though no bow is visible), left arm supporting posture. Legs gripping horse tightly, feet positioned for stability.
persons.tactic: Archery preparation — likely about to shoot from horseback, using momentum for accuracy.

persons.posture: Seated under canopy, left hand resting on knee, right hand holding object near chest; body upright, facing forward with slight turn toward center; weight balanced evenly on both legs.
persons.tactic: Representing a commanding officer or strategist observing battlefield preparations; posture conveys authority and calm control during tactical review.

persons.posture: Seated opposite central figure, hands clasped in lap, head slightly bowed; feet tucked beneath seat, back straight; weight centered over hips.
persons.tactic: Likely subordinate or advisor, demonstrating deference while participating in strategic discussion.

persons.posture: Seated prominently under canopy, hands raised slightly as if gesturing or explaining; legs crossed or bent with feet flat; torso erect, head turned slightly to left; weight distributed forward for emphasis.
persons.tactic: Commander delivering orders or doctrine; the focal point of the scene, indicating leadership and decision-making role.

persons.posture: Standing behind seated figure, one hand raised near shoulder, other hand at side; feet shoulder-width apart, body angled slightly forward; weight shifted to front leg.
persons.tactic: Attendant or messenger ready to act on command; posture suggests alertness and readiness to serve.

persons.posture: Standing beside tree, one hand extended outward, other hand held low; legs apart, knees slightly bent; body leaning slightly forward with gaze directed toward central group.
persons.tactic: Observer or scout reporting information; spatial positioning indicates vigilance and communication role.

persons.posture: Partially obscured, seated with hands folded in lap, head tilted downward; legs folded beneath body; weight settled low for stability.
persons.tactic: Supporting official or scribe recording proceedings; passive but essential presence in administrative or ceremonial context.

images.original_text:
戰場圖說
左：騎兵衝鋒，右：步兵列陣，中：飛石陣，遠山為背景，旗幟飄揚。

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, leaning forward with left leg extended, right hand gripping spear pointed ahead, body angled low for speed and momentum, weight distributed forward to maintain balance during charge.
persons.tactic: Rapid cavalry assault to break enemy formation.

persons.posture: Standing in tight line, feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, left arm holding shield upright, right hand gripping sword raised at shoulder height, head turned left scanning enemy approach.
persons.tactic: Defensive phalanx formation to absorb initial cavalry冲击 and counterattack.

persons.posture: Kneeling behind foliage cover, left knee on ground, right leg bent, both hands drawing back a sling loaded with stones, eyes focused ahead, body coiled for explosive release.
persons.tactic: Indirect ranged harassment using slingshot to disrupt advancing forces.

persons.posture: Mid-air fall from horse, legs splayed, arms flailing, helmet askew, horse galloping past below, indicating loss of control or weapon strike.
persons.tactic: Victim of ambush or flank attack; demonstrates vulnerability of mounted troops.

persons.posture: Crouched behind shrubbery, bow drawn, left foot forward, right foot back for stability, arrow nocked and aimed diagonally upward toward distant target, gaze fixed on aiming point.
persons.tactic: Ambush archery from concealed position to weaken enemy advance.

persons.posture: Standing atop rocky outcrop, one hand raised signaling, other hand resting on hip, wearing armor with visible collar and belt, facing battlefield with commanding stance.
persons.tactic: Commander directing troop movements from elevated vantage point.

persons.posture: Riding a horse, leaning forward with right arm extended backward as if drawing a bow; left leg bent, right leg extended for balance; body angled slightly left, weight shifted to rear hoof; head turned toward target.
persons.tactic: Archery in motion — preparing to shoot while riding at speed, demonstrating mounted archery technique.

persons.posture: On horseback, leaning back with both arms raised, holding reins; legs gripping horse’s sides, torso upright; eyes focused ahead, suggesting command or pursuit.
persons.tactic: Commanding or leading cavalry charge; maintaining control of mount while advancing in formation.

persons.posture: Flying through air, limbs splayed, one arm reaching forward, legs trailing; body horizontal, suggesting aerial leap or fall from horse; no visible weapon.
persons.tactic: Acrobatic evasion or dismount maneuver during combat; possibly simulating a fall after being struck.

persons.posture: In mid-air, legs bent, arms outstretched as if bracing for impact; body tilted downward, facing ground; appears to be falling or jumping from height.
persons.tactic: Aerial descent or escape technique — possibly evading attack by leaping off terrain or mount.

persons.posture: On horseback, leaning sideways with sword drawn and raised above head; left hand gripping reins, right arm swinging downward in strike motion; legs braced for momentum.
persons.tactic: Cavalry sword strike in motion — executing an overhead slash while riding at speed, likely targeting enemy rider.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky terrain near water, facing right with left foot forward, right foot back, body slightly crouched. Right hand holds a staff raised high above shoulder, left hand extended forward in a defensive or preparatory grip. Weight distributed mostly on front leg, ready to strike or parry.
persons.tactic: Preparatory stance for striking or defending against an approaching opponent; likely part of a martial sequence involving staff combat.

persons.posture: Mounted on horse, facing left, holding reins with left hand, right arm extended downward holding a staff. Body leans slightly forward, eyes focused ahead. Horse is in mid-stride, legs positioned for movement across terrain.
persons.tactic: Riding attack or pursuit maneuver using staff while mounted; suggests cavalry-based martial application or battlefield engagement.

persons.posture: On foot, standing behind the mounted figure, holding a staff horizontally across chest with both hands. Feet apart in stable stance, head turned toward the direction of movement. Appears to be supporting or coordinating with the rider.
persons.tactic: Supporting role in formation; possibly providing cover or signaling during coordinated attack or retreat.

persons.posture: Standing atop a fortified gate structure, holding a staff vertically with one hand, looking down at the scene below. Legs apart, torso upright, weight balanced evenly. Appears to be observing or commanding.
persons.tactic: Commanding or surveillance position; may indicate strategic oversight of the battle or martial exercise.

persons.posture: Partially visible behind the gate, standing near the entrance, holding a staff diagonally across body. Head turned toward the action, feet planted firmly. Appears to be part of the garrison or guard force.
persons.tactic: Defensive or auxiliary role; likely prepared to reinforce or respond to threats emerging from the riverbank area.

images.original_text: 右賊犯畿南關賊自河北散後大隊俱赴五臺來由五臺犯畿南准樞部暨保定丁撫督移速以京營兵北援而京兵適搜涉縣山中賊自晉返豫飛檄令其介馬北馳余患盛武空虛十一月初七日移駐彰德次日巡道常道立報賊由畿南復犯武安林涉三縣共結十有三營京營兵亦尾賊而南矣余乘夜抵林縣值賊大隊將逼城余僅率丁壯數十人守城官兵且信且疑門不敢開賊哨聞余已至城下稍稍解退而余始得入城

鄉里圖說
山野行軍圖
左：騎馬將士持旗引路，後隨步兵數人。
右：山徑崎嶇，數卒負糧艙而行，旁有樵夫或坐或立。
全圖表現鄉間山路行軍之景，人物動作各異，具戰備與勞作之態。

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, holding a large banner or flagpole upright with both hands, body slightly leaning forward, legs gripping the saddle, weight centered over the horse’s back. The horse is in mid-stride, moving uphill along a winding path.
persons.tactic: Leading the column during a military march through mountainous terrain; using the banner to mark direction and maintain unit cohesion.

persons.posture: Walking behind the banner-bearer, one soldier carrying a large pack on his back, another walking beside him with a staff or spear held diagonally across the body. Both are ascending a steep slope, knees bent, shoulders hunched for balance.
persons.tactic: Logistical support and rear guard during mountain march; maintaining formation while navigating difficult terrain.

persons.posture: Three soldiers walking single-file along a ridge, each carrying a bundle or pack. The lead figure looks ahead, the middle one glances back, and the trailing one holds his head high. All are in a steady walking stance with feet planted firmly on uneven ground.
persons.tactic: Scouting or patrolling along elevated terrain to monitor surroundings during campaign movement.

persons.posture: Sitting or crouching near the trail, possibly resting or observing. One hand rests on knee, other arm extended toward the path. Head turned sideways as if watching approaching troops.
persons.tactic: Reconnaissance or ambush preparation from concealed position along route.

persons.posture: Standing beside the seated figure, holding a long object (possibly staff or weapon) vertically. Body angled slightly backward, feet apart for stability on slope.
persons.tactic: Guarding or signaling to rear elements of the column; maintaining vigilance against potential threats from below.

persons.posture: Crouched or kneeling beside the path, appearing to rest or tend to equipment. One hand on ground, head lowered, suggesting fatigue or repair activity.
persons.tactic: Resting point or maintenance stop during prolonged march; possibly adjusting gear or tending to wounded.

images.original_text: 山石嶙峋，松柏苍劲，溪流蜿蜒。此图描绘自然山水之景，无武术人物或器械。

persons.posture: Standing on a rocky path, left foot forward, right foot back; holding a staff horizontally with both hands at waist level; body slightly crouched, facing left; weight balanced between feet, ready to advance or defend.
persons.tactic: Defensive stance with staff, preparing to intercept an incoming attack from the left.

persons.posture: Riding a horse, leaning forward with right arm extended holding a spear pointed downward; left hand gripping reins; legs straddling the horse, knees bent for stability; head turned toward the front, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Cavalry charge with spear thrust, aiming to pierce or disrupt enemy formation.

persons.posture: On foot, standing upright with left leg slightly forward; right hand raised holding a sword above shoulder, blade angled downward; left hand near hip, palm open; torso straight, gaze fixed forward.
persons.tactic: Preparation for a vertical slash or counterattack, maintaining balance for immediate strike.

persons.posture: Kneeling beside a fallen figure, one knee on ground, upper body bent forward; right hand gripping a spear shaft near the tip, left hand supporting the ground; head lowered, appearing to assist or secure the fallen.
persons.tactic: Assisting ally or securing weapon after combat, possibly tending to wounded or controlling battlefield perimeter.

persons.posture: Riding horse, left hand gripping reins, right hand raised holding sword, body leaning forward slightly, weight distributed forward to maintain balance during movement across uneven terrain.
persons.tactic: Cavalry charge or reconnaissance advance, preparing for engagement while maintaining control of mount.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky slope, one leg bent in low stance, left arm extended holding spear pointed downward, right arm drawn back as if preparing to thrust or parry, torso angled for stability and readiness.
persons.tactic: Defensive positioning on elevated ground, ready to counterattack or block incoming assault with spear.

persons.posture: Kneeling on ground near water’s edge, both hands gripping staff horizontally, shoulders squared, eyes focused ahead, weight centered over knees for stability and power generation.
persons.tactic: Ground-based defense or ambush technique using staff to intercept approaching enemy from front or flank.

persons.posture: Lying prone behind rock outcrop, right arm extended holding bow drawn, left knee tucked, body aligned parallel to ground for concealment and accuracy, head turned toward target.
persons.tactic: Sniper-style archery position, utilizing terrain cover to launch surprise attack without revealing position.

persons.posture: On foot, standing upright with feet shoulder-width apart, left hand holding shield raised at chest level, right hand gripping short sword in guard position, body slightly crouched for mobility.
persons.tactic: Shield-and-sword defensive formation, prepared to absorb blows while closing distance for counter-strike.

images.original_text: 此圖為山野戰場之景，左上角一人騎馬疾馳，右下角一人持械立於溪畔。山巒起伏，草木叢生，石塊散佈，地形險峻。

persons.posture: Riding a horse in a galloping stance, body leaning forward, legs gripping the saddle, arms extended for balance, weight distributed forward to maintain momentum across uneven terrain.
persons.tactic: Rapid cavalry maneuver, likely advancing or retreating under pressure, using terrain advantage to evade or pursue.

persons.posture: Standing in a stable combat stance near a stream, feet planted wide apart for stability, torso upright with slight forward lean, hands positioned near waist as if holding an unseen weapon or preparing to draw one.
persons.tactic: Defensive or ambush position, utilizing natural cover and elevation of the riverbank to observe or engage approaching forces.

images.original_text: 右三路擊賊圖方余駐雒城賊勢頗張欲繇東北當是時左良玉先於十一月二十八日率師赴援永盧等處鄧兵於十二月十三日抵雒陽山西將李卓帥兵亦於十三日至余分布三路合力夾擊俱於十四日鼓勇而前賊之向嵩廬未寧者左良玉擊敗於青山嶺銀銅溝賊之向滬池者鄧李擊敗於硤石仍一面各移陝郎兩撫發兵堵截

劍術圖說
左為敵，右為我。敵持劍而進，我側身避之，隨即反擊。
山巒疊嶂，溪流蜿蜒，數人於此間搏鬥，動作迅捷，氣勢雄健。

persons.posture: Standing in a forward advancing stance, left foot forward, right foot back; body slightly crouched and turned sideways to the opponent; right hand holds sword extended forward in a thrusting motion; left hand is drawn back near the hip for balance. Weight distributed 60% on front leg, 40% on rear leg.
persons.tactic: Executing a sidestep evasion followed by a counter-thrust strike — a classic defensive-offensive maneuver in swordsmanship.

persons.posture: In a lateral retreat stance, feet wide apart, body angled away from the attacker; right arm raised with sword held high in guard position; left arm bent across chest for protection. Weight balanced evenly between both legs, ready to pivot or leap.
persons.tactic: Defensive parry and retreat, preparing to reposition for a counterattack — demonstrating tactical awareness and spatial control.

persons.posture: Running forward with left leg extended, right leg trailing; arms pumping for momentum; head turned backward to observe pursuer. Body leans forward in aggressive pursuit stance.
persons.tactic: Chasing down an opponent, likely attempting to close distance for a surprise strike or flank attack.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, left leg bent under body, right leg extended behind; upper torso twisted to the side as if recoiling from impact; right hand clutching abdomen, suggesting injury or fall.
persons.tactic: Receiving a blow or being knocked down — indicating a failed defense or tactical misstep during combat.

images.original_text:
單見圖說
兵家之術，莫先於戰馬。馬者，軍中之利器也。騎射之法，尤為精妙。圖中所繪，乃戰場奔襲之景。騎士馳騁山巒之間，箭矢如雨，敵陣崩潰。此為《武經總要》所載戰術圖解。

persons.posture: Rider on horse, leaning forward in aggressive charge, right hand drawing bowstring back, left hand gripping reins; legs braced for balance, body angled low to reduce wind resistance; weight distributed forward over horse’s neck.
persons.tactic: Archery while riding — a classic cavalry tactic known as 'riding and shooting' (騎射), used to harass enemy formations before close combat.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, mid-gallop with spear held horizontally across body, right arm extended forward, left arm bent for balance; horse’s head lowered, ears back indicating speed; body leans slightly backward to counter momentum.
persons.tactic: Spear thrusting from horseback — a shock attack technique designed to break enemy lines or disrupt formations during pursuit.

persons.posture: Rider on horse, turning sharply to the left, right arm raised holding a curved blade (possibly saber), left hand guiding horse’s mane; legs tucked inward for maneuverability; horse’s hooves kicking up dust, indicating rapid directional change.
persons.tactic: Cavalry flanking maneuver with sword — used to encircle or strike vulnerable sides of enemy units after initial disruption by archers and spearmen.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, leaning forward with right arm extended, left hand gripping reins; horse in mid-stride, head lowered, legs in dynamic motion; body angled slightly forward, weight shifted toward front of saddle.
persons.tactic: Equestrian combat stance, likely preparing to strike or evade while mounted; suggests mobility and control in battlefield maneuvering.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky terrain, one leg forward in a low stance, arms raised in defensive or preparatory striking position; torso turned sideways, eyes focused forward; appears ready to engage or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Ground-based martial posture, possibly a defensive block or initiating offensive movement; indicates readiness for close-quarters combat in rugged terrain.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky terrain, facing left, right arm extended holding a sword pointed downward, left arm bent at side, weight shifted slightly forward, body angled to present profile to viewer, suggesting readiness for combat or ceremonial stance.
persons.tactic: Martial application appears to be a defensive or preparatory posture with sword drawn, possibly illustrating a moment of confrontation or strategic positioning in warfare.

persons.posture: Standing beside the first figure, partially obscured, holding a banner or standard aloft with right hand, left arm bent, feet planted firmly, body upright and facing forward — suggesting command or heraldic role rather than direct combat.
persons.tactic: This figure likely represents a military commander or flag-bearer, indicating troop formation or battlefield leadership rather than individual martial technique.

persons.posture: Sitting on a rocky outcrop, legs crossed, body slightly leaning forward, hands resting on knees. Weight distributed evenly, head turned slightly to the right, observing the scene.
persons.tactic: Positioned for observation or waiting, possibly guarding a passage or preparing for ambush. Stance suggests patience and readiness.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, upper body upright, left hand extended forward as if pointing or signaling, right hand resting on thigh. Eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Signaling or directing others; may be coordinating movement or indicating danger. Tactical communication in a group setting.

persons.posture: Squatting low with back straight, arms bent at elbows, hands held near chest in a defensive or preparatory stance. Feet wide apart for stability.
persons.tactic: Preparation for combat or evasion; likely anticipating an attack or maneuvering into position for defense or counterattack.

persons.posture: Standing upright, facing leftward, one foot slightly forward, arms relaxed at sides. Head tilted slightly upward, gaze directed toward the cliff face.
persons.tactic: Scouting or surveying terrain; possibly assessing the environment for strategic advantage or enemy approach.

persons.posture: Riding horse, left leg extended forward, right leg back, body leaning slightly forward, holding spear pointed diagonally upward with right hand, left hand gripping horse reins. Weight distributed forward to maintain balance during movement.
persons.tactic: Charge attack formation — advancing with spear to break enemy lines.

persons.posture: On foot, standing in a low horse stance, left foot forward, right foot back, torso twisted slightly left, right arm raised high holding spear vertically, left arm bent at elbow supporting spear shaft. Eyes focused ahead, ready to strike.
persons.tactic: Defensive counter-strike — preparing to parry or thrust when enemy closes distance.

persons.posture: Mounted on horse, facing right, both hands gripping bow drawn back, arrow nocked and aimed straight ahead. Legs braced for stability, shoulders squared, head turned toward target. Weight centered over saddle.
persons.tactic: Long-range archery support — providing covering fire while cavalry advances.

persons.posture: On foot, crouched low with knees bent, left hand holding spear shaft near base, right hand gripping spear tip pointing downward. Torso angled sideways, eyes scanning ground for ambush or terrain advantage.
persons.tactic: Ambush or terrain-based defense — using low profile to avoid detection while preparing to stab or block.

persons.posture: Riding horse, right arm extended backward holding spear horizontally, left arm gripping reins. Horse in gallop, legs mid-stride, body leaning into turn. Head turned left, observing flanking movement.
persons.tactic: Flanking maneuver — circling around enemy rear to disrupt formation or create distraction.

images.original_text:
刺虎圖己
山巒起伏，雲霧繚繞。左側一人持長兵器，作刺虎之勢；右側二人，一騎馬者揮刀而下，一立於石上者張臂如搏虎狀。遠處有飛鳥掠空，近處水波微動，地形崎嶇。

persons.posture: Stands in a forward lunging stance, left leg extended, right knee bent; holds a spear pointed diagonally downward toward the ground; body leans slightly forward with weight distributed 60% on front leg, 40% on back; head turned to gaze at target ahead.
persons.tactic: Executing a piercing thrust technique against a tiger, using momentum from the lunge to drive the spear into the opponent’s vital area.

persons.posture: Mounted on horse, leaning forward with right arm raised high holding a sword; left hand gripping reins; legs clenched around horse’s flank for stability; body angled slightly backward to maintain balance during descent.
persons.tactic: Performing a descending slash strike aimed at the tiger’s neck or shoulder, utilizing the height and speed of the horse for increased force.

persons.posture: Balanced on one foot atop a rocky outcrop, arms wide open as if embracing or grappling with an unseen beast; torso twisted slightly to the left; knees bent for shock absorption; eyes fixed forward with intense focus.
persons.tactic: Employing a close-quarters grappling or blocking technique, preparing to seize or disarm the tiger by controlling its limbs or head with simultaneous hand movements.

persons.posture: In mid-air, legs bent, arms flailing outward as if falling or leaping; body horizontal with slight rotation; appears to be either dodging or attacking from above.
persons.tactic: Executing an aerial maneuver—possibly a leap attack or evasive roll—to disrupt the tiger’s positioning or strike from an unexpected angle.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, left leg forward, right leg back, body leaning slightly forward, holding bow with right hand, arrow nocked and drawn, aiming leftward. Horse in mid-gallop, head turned left, ears alert, tail raised.
persons.tactic: Archery attack from horseback, targeting an enemy ahead. Emphasizes mobility and ranged strike.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky ground, left foot forward, right foot back, knees bent, torso upright. Right arm extended forward holding a spear pointed at target; left arm held back for balance. Eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: Spear thrusting technique, advancing to close combat distance while maintaining defensive posture.

persons.posture: Kneeling or crouching behind rock, left knee on ground, right leg bent, upper body low and angled left. Right hand gripping staff horizontally across chest, left hand bracing against rock. Head turned right, observing surroundings.
persons.tactic: Defensive ambush or counterattack position, using terrain for cover while preparing to strike with staff.

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot forward, right foot back; body slightly crouched; right hand holding sword raised above shoulder, left hand extended forward in guard position; weight balanced on both legs with emphasis on front leg.
persons.tactic: Offensive sword strike targeting opponent’s upper torso or head, preparing for follow-up thrust or parry.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, leaning forward with left arm extended to hold spear pointed downward; right arm bent at elbow, hand near chest; body angled to the side for balance and evasion.
persons.tactic: Defensive spear posture to block incoming attack while preparing to counter-strike upward or sideways.

persons.posture: Riding horse, leaning leftward with right arm swinging staff downward in arc; left arm gripping reins; legs straddling horse with knees bent for stability.
persons.tactic: Mounted staff strike aimed at dismounting or striking enemy below horse level.

images.original_text:
馬上戰法
城下騎兵交鋒圖
左為敵軍，右為我軍。敵軍三騎持長矛突進，我軍一騎持弓回射，城樓旌旗飄揚，烽煙四起。

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, leaning forward with left leg extended, right hand gripping a spear pointed diagonally forward. Body angled slightly to the left, weight shifted forward for momentum in charge.
persons.tactic: Frontal cavalry assault using spear to pierce enemy lines.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, body upright, right arm extended holding a spear straight ahead. Horse in mid-gallop, legs bent for speed and control. Eyes focused forward, preparing for engagement.
persons.tactic: Supporting spear charge, maintaining formation while advancing toward enemy fortifications.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, leaning sideways to the right, spear held low and angled backward as if preparing to strike or parry. Head turned to observe surroundings, horse’s head lowered in motion.
persons.tactic: Flanking maneuver or defensive counterattack, using spear to disrupt enemy formation.

persons.posture: Rider on horseback, seated upright, drawing bow with left hand while right hand holds arrow nocked. Back arched slightly, eyes fixed on target ahead. Horse stationary or moving slowly, allowing accurate shot.
persons.tactic: Archery support from mounted position, targeting enemy riders or gate defenders.

images.original_text:
劍戯圖己
左有飛仙，右有弓手。飛仙持劍，騰空而起；弓手張弓，立於崖邊。水波瀾漫，山巒疊嶂，雲氣繚繞。

persons.posture: Flying through the air with flowing robes, one leg extended backward, sword held in right hand pointed downward, body angled diagonally upward, weight shifted forward as if leaping or descending from height.
persons.tactic: Aerial sword strike technique, possibly representing a flying leap or skyward descent to attack from above.

persons.posture: Standing on rocky cliff edge, left foot forward, right foot back for stability, bow drawn with both hands, arrow nocked and aimed horizontally toward the left, body slightly crouched, eyes focused on target.
persons.tactic: Archery ambush technique — positioning at high ground to shoot down or intercept an airborne opponent.

images.original_text:
陣具圖說
兵戈交鋒，騎卒奔突，步卒奮戰於河岸。或持長槍，或握短刃，或躍馬揚鞭，或倒地受創。水岸崎嶇，塵土飛揚，戰局激烈。

persons.posture: Riding horse, left leg extended forward, right leg back, body leaning slightly forward, holding spear in right hand pointed diagonally upward, left hand gripping reins. Weight distributed forward to maintain balance during charge.
persons.tactic: Charging attack with spear, aiming to pierce enemy line while mounted.

persons.posture: On foot, standing in a low horse stance, left leg bent, right leg straight, torso twisted slightly right, both hands gripping sword in front of chest, eyes focused ahead. Weight balanced evenly between legs for stability and readiness to strike.
persons.tactic: Defensive stance preparing to counterattack with sword; ready to parry or slash depending on opponent’s movement.

persons.posture: Lying prone on ground, arms outstretched, legs bent at knees, head turned left, appearing injured or fallen. Body angled diagonally across the battlefield, suggesting collapse after being struck.
persons.tactic: Fallen combatant — indicates vulnerability after failed defense or attack.

persons.posture: Mid-air leap, legs tucked, arms reaching forward as if throwing weapon or preparing to land in combat stance. Torso rotated, weight centered over center of gravity for controlled descent.
persons.tactic: Aerial assault or evasive maneuver to avoid attack while positioning for counterstrike.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, left arm raised holding shield, right arm swinging sword downward in arc. Head turned toward incoming threat, eyes narrowed. Weight shifted onto supporting knee for stability.
persons.tactic: Shield-and-sword combination technique — defend against overhead blow while preparing to stab or slash.

persons.posture: Running forward with left leg stepping ahead, right leg trailing, arms pumping for momentum. Torso upright, gaze fixed ahead. Weight shifting dynamically from rear to front foot with each stride.
persons.tactic: Rapid advance to close distance with enemy, possibly to initiate melee or flank attack.

persons.posture: Horseback rider, right hand drawing bowstring, left hand holding reins, body leaning backward to brace against draw tension. Eyes aimed at distant target, feet braced in stirrups.
persons.tactic: Mounted archery — firing arrow while riding, requiring balance and precision under motion.

persons.posture: Falling backward, legs splayed, arms flailing, head tilted up. Appears struck by projectile or weapon. No defensive posture — indicates sudden defeat.
persons.tactic: Victim of surprise attack or overwhelming force; no tactical intent remaining.

persons.posture: Standing upright, facing opponent, left foot forward, right foot back, hands clasped around staff held horizontally across waist. Shoulders relaxed, knees slightly bent for agility.
persons.tactic: Staff guard position — prepared to block or sweep opponent’s weapon while maintaining distance.

persons.posture: Crouched low, one knee on ground, other leg bent, staff planted vertically beside body for support. Head lowered, eyes scanning ground level — likely anticipating low attacks.
persons.tactic: Low-strike defense or ambush setup — ready to rise and strike when enemy approaches within reach.

persons.posture: Jumping sideways, left leg extended behind, right leg bent beneath body, arms swung wide for momentum. Torso twisted mid-air, looking toward direction of jump.
persons.tactic: Evasive sidestep to dodge incoming strike or reposition for counterattack.

persons.posture: Sitting on ground, legs crossed, holding dagger in right hand near mouth as if about to bite it — symbolic gesture of despair or last-ditch resistance. Left hand clutching robe.
persons.tactic: Psychological or ritualistic act — may represent surrender, defiance, or suicide rather than active combat.

persons.posture: Running uphill, arms pumping, legs driving powerfully, head down focused on path. Body leans forward aggressively, indicating urgency or pursuit.
persons.tactic: Pursuit or retreat under pressure — using terrain advantage to gain speed or escape danger.

persons.posture: Standing still, facing left, holding halberd with both hands above shoulder, blade angled downward. Feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly flexed — ready to thrust or swing.
persons.tactic: Heavy weapon preparation — poised to deliver decisive blow against armored or grouped opponents.

persons.posture: Reeling backward from impact, arms thrown up defensively, legs buckling, eyes closed — clearly struck by weapon. Body contorts mid-fall.
persons.tactic: Defensive failure — unable to withstand direct hit; likely eliminated from combat.

persons.posture: Riding horse, left leg extended forward, right leg back, body leaning slightly forward, holding spear pointed diagonally upward; left hand gripping reins, right hand raised in striking motion.
persons.tactic: Spear thrusting attack from horseback, targeting enemy’s upper torso or head.

persons.posture: On foot, crouched stance with knees bent, left arm raised to parry, right arm drawn back to strike; weight balanced on front foot, eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: Defensive counter-strike using short sword after deflecting incoming blow.

persons.posture: Mounted archer, seated sideways on horse, drawing bowstring with right hand, left arm steadying the bow; legs wrapped around horse’s flank for stability.
persons.tactic: Ranged attack while mounted, aiming at distant target during cavalry charge.
